text:
, still it came to possess,
as it were, the charm of a lake in autumn with its waves, white
lotuses and swans, as a result of its being filled with floundering
whales, pearl-oysters and conch-shells.

Z: ll^ll
The sage transformed the ocean into the sky, as it had white
clouds in the form of whales, stars in the form of gems, the Moon
in the form of crystal gem, the autumnal splendour in the form of
the waterless bed and comets (Ketus) in the form of the rays of the
gems on the hoods of the serpents.
[The poet has employed a fine, complete metaphor in addition
to alliteration. The metre is

154 Brhat Samhitd
The poet now proceeds to describe in prose the Vindhya
mountain and its treatment at the hands of the sage.
ii^II
Listen, ye, now to the rising of that sage (Agastya or
Canopus), who stemmed the Vindhya Mountain, which threatened
to obstruct the course of the Sun's carriage by raising its peaks;
